agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A feature request to add a 'confirmed' status to the Quotes bounded context. The term 'confirmed' already exists in the Orders bounded context with a precise, established meaning that includes payment completion and immutability. The proposed usage in Quotes explicitly contradicts this — it means verbal agreement WITHOUT payment.","bounded_context":"Quotes (proposed change) conflicting with Orders (established)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Confirmed Order","definition":"ESTABLISHED: An order that has been paid and is queued for fulfillment. It is immutable — no further changes are permitted."},{"term":"Confirmed Quote (proposed)","definition":"PROPOSED: A quote where a customer has verbally agreed to terms but payment has NOT yet occurred. This is mutable (payment still pending, customer could still back out)."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A sales agent tells support 'the customer is confirmed' — support cannot distinguish whether this means paid-and-queued (order) or verbal-agreement-only (quote)","expected_behavior":"The system and its human operators must never be ambiguous about payment status. A single term cannot mean both 'paid' and 'unpaid' across contexts."},{"scenario":"Reporting dashboard shows 'confirmed' items — finance assumes these are revenue-bearing (paid), but half are actually unpaid verbal agreements","expected_behavior":"Revenue reporting becomes unreliable. Financial audits flag discrepancies."},{"scenario":"Integration or API consumers filter by status='confirmed' expecting paid orders, and receive unpaid quotes in mixed results","expected_behavior":"Downstream systems make incorrect fulfillment or billing decisions based on ambiguous status."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"HIGH RISK — Overloading 'confirmed' to mean both 'paid' and 'unpaid' will corrupt revenue reporting. Finance teams, auditors, and automated billing systems will misinterpret quote confirmations as paid orders.","operational":"Team communication breaks down. When someone says 'this is confirmed,' every participant must now ask 'confirmed-confirmed or quote-confirmed?' This is a classic domain language corruption that compounds over time.","user":"Sales agents get a workflow improvement (tracking verbal agreements), but the terminology collision creates confusion for support, ops, and finance teams who already use 'confirmed' to mean payment-complete.","risk":"Domain language pollution is one of the highest-cost mistakes in system design. It starts as a convenience and becomes a permanent source of bugs, miscommunication, and incorrect business decisions. The cost to fix grows exponentially once the term is embedded in code, APIs, reports, and team vocabulary.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Sales Agents","impact":"Positive — they get a way to track verbal agreements. But the status name is the problem, not the capability."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Negative — 'confirmed' currently guarantees payment. Redefining it to include unpaid items breaks their mental model and reporting filters."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Negative — cannot quickly determine payment status from the word 'confirmed' alone."},{"group":"Engineering / API Consumers","impact":"Negative — status enum collision across contexts creates integration bugs and requires defensive disambiguation everywhere."},{"group":"Operations / Fulfillment","impact":"Negative — risk of premature fulfillment if a 'confirmed' quote is mistaken for a 'confirmed' order."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction: 'confirmed' is defined as paid+immutable in the established domain, and the feature request explicitly defines it as unpaid+mutable. This is not an ambiguity — it is a named contradiction. Confidence is low because shipping this as-is would be domain-incorrect regardless of technical implementation quality.","high_confidence_areas":["The term collision is real and will cause cross-context confusion","The business need (tracking verbal agreements) is legitimate","The problem is the naming, not the capability"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the organization has other contexts already using 'confirmed' beyond Orders","What the full quote lifecycle looks like (are there other statuses already defined?)"],"assumptions":["The Orders definition of 'confirmed' is authoritative and widely understood across teams","Quotes and Orders share reporting infrastructure, APIs, or user interfaces where the term overlap would surface"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"CRITICAL DOMAIN LANGUAGE CONFLICT: The term 'confirmed' has an established, precise meaning in the Orders context: paid + immutable + queued for fulfillment. The feature request redefines 'confirmed' in the Quotes context to mean the exact opposite on two dimensions: unpaid and mutable. This is not a matter of interpretation — the spec explicitly states 'payment has not yet occurred' while the established definition requires payment completion. A single ubiquitous term cannot simultaneously mean 'paid' and 'not paid' across the business.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance/Accounting","Sales Agents","Customer Support","Engineering","Operations/Fulfillment"],"options":["REJECT the term 'confirmed' for quotes. Use a distinct term that accurately reflects the business state: 'verbally_accepted', 'customer_agreed', 'pending_payment', or 'approved_by_customer'.","Namespace the statuses explicitly in all contexts: 'quote_confirmed' vs 'order_confirmed'. This is a band-aid — it admits the term is overloaded rather than fixing it.","Redefine 'confirmed' globally to mean 'agreed upon' and add a separate 'paid' status to orders. This is high-cost and breaks the established invariant."],"recommendation":"Option A — Use a distinct term. The business need is real: sales agents need to track verbal agreements. The solution is a properly named status like 'accepted' or 'verbally_agreed' that communicates exactly what happened without polluting the established meaning of 'confirmed'. The capability ships, the domain stays clean."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Ubiquitous language collision: 'confirmed' cannot mean 'paid and immutable' in Orders and 'unpaid and mutable' in Quotes within the same business domain.","business_rule_broken":"A confirmed order has been paid and is queued for fulfillment. A confirmed order is immutable."}]}
